most_recent_game.recap_text:
NASHVILLE, Tenn. -
WKU Baseball (13-8, 3-0 CUSA) defeated Belmont (8-13) 7-2 on Wednesday evening at First Horizon Park in Nashville, Tenn.
HOW IT HAPPENED
In the top of the first the Hilltoppers loaded the bases and
JP Acosta
reached on a fielder's choice that scored the leadoff man
Austin Haller
to give WKU a 1-0 lead.
Hayden Robbins
picked up from a career day Sunday and laced a double down the left field line to score
Camden Ross
and put WKU up two runs.
With two outs in the second and runners on first and third,
Lane Arroyos
drove out his fifth home run of the year on an 0-1 count to make it a five-run Hilltopper lead.
After a quiet few innings, back-to-back singles for the Hilltoppers put runners on first and second as Arroyos grounded out to put both into scoring position.
Kyle Hayes
then flew out to the right fielder to score
Austin Haller
and make it a 6-0 game.
A wild pitch scored Ross and opened up the WKU advantage to 7-0 in the top half of the sixth inning.
The Bruins scored their first run of the game on a passed ball on a dropped third strike to make it a 7-1 game in the bottom of the seventh. Belmont added a run in the eighth to make it a five-run game, ultimately leading to the final score of 7-2.
NOTABLES
With the win WKU improves to 13-8 (3-0 CUSA) on the year.
WKU has now won five straight games, their longest streak of the season.
The win marked the 30th for WKU in their 56 game history with Belmont. The Hilltoppers now hold a 30-26 series lead.
Ka'olu Holt
started for the Hilltoppers on Wednesday. He finished with three strikeouts and two walks in 1.0 inning of work.
Holt earned his first win of the season and is now 1-1 on the year.
WKU used nine relievers on Wednesday in
Bryson Vawter
, Kane Samuels,
Gage Olsen
,
Roman Petricca
,
Conner Wolf
,
Mac McGarry
,
Sam Frizzi
,
Declan Francis
, and
Jacob O'Day
.
Vawter and Petricca both made their first appearances as Hilltoppers on Wednesday. Vawter tossed 1.0 innings and faced three batters and Petricca tossed 0.2 innings and struck out one batter.
Jacob O'Day
closed out the game for WKU and struck two batters in 1.0 inning pitched.
Hilltopper pitchers combined to toss 11 strikeouts on Wednesday. WKU has now done so in 10 games this season.
Lane Arroyos
recorded three RBIs for the third time this season and the second time in the last week. Arroyos finished 1-for-5 along with his three RBIs and one run.
Arroyos also powered his fifth home run of the year, a 3-run home run to right field, to put the Hilltoppers up 5-0 in the second inning.
Camden Ross
went 2-for-2 on Wednesday to lead the Hilltoppers in the category and finished with three runs scored.
Austin Haller
went 1-for-3 on Wednesday and scored three runs.
Hayden Robbins
finished the day 1-for-1 with one RBI.
NEXT UP
WKU will travel to Jacksonville, Ala. for a three game series with Jax State. First pitch Friday, March 20 is set for 6 p.m. CT and will be streamed on ESPN+.
How to follow WKU Baseball:
For complete information on WKU Hilltopper Baseball, visit WKUSports.com or follow the program via social media @WKU_Baseball on X and @WKUBaseball on Instagram.
